commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=3af74c547fa9acedbbf13bbde9cdc29d92350ac3)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a pending channel-reload abort flag called from SIGUSR1/in-process restart paths, updates deferred channel reload checks, and also changes shared reply dispatch for Telegram reasoning payloads.

PR surface: Source +56. Total +56 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main lets deferred channel reloads resume into stop/start while shutdown does not abort or drain the active config reloader before channel teardown. I did not run a live Telegram webhook EADDRINUSE reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blockers require contributor/maintainer handling because the branch needs scope cleanup, a lifecycle-layer fix choice, and stronger real behavior proof before merge.

target.title: fix(reload): cancel deferred channel reload on in-process restart
target.body:
## Summary

When SIGUSR1 fires during a deferred channel reload (triggered by a config change requiring channel restart), both code paths race to start the same channel. The deferred reload polls for active work to drain, then starts the channel — but the in-process restart also starts the channel. On Telegram, this double-spawns the webhook listener and the loser gets `EADDRINUSE`, entering a permanent crash loop with up to 10 auto-restart attempts.

This PR adds an abort flag checked by the deferred reload poll loop, and sets it from `resetGatewayRestartStateForInProcessRestart` via a lazy import. When the in-process restart fires, any waiting deferred reload aborts immediately — the full restart subsumes it anyway.

Fixes #79487


## Fixes since last review

**P1 fix — return cancelled result from waitForActiveWork**: `waitForActiveWorkBeforeChannelReload` now returns `boolean` (true = cancelled by in-process restart). Both call sites (`stopChannelsBeforePluginReplace` and the non-plugin channel reload path) check the return value and skip channel stop/start operations when cancelled. Previously the function returned void and callers always proceeded into channel operations, so the abort flag was observed but did not actually prevent the race.

**P1 fix — abort at SIGUSR1 reception, not next-start**: The SIGUSR1 handler in `run-loop.ts` now calls `abortPendingChannelReloads()` immediately via dynamic import, before calling `request("restart", ...)`. Previously the abort was deferred to the next-start lifecycle boundary (`resetGatewayRestartStateForInProcessRestart` in `onIteration`), which runs after the old gateway has already shut down — too late to catch a deferred reload that starts channel operations during shutdown drain.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: Deferred channel reload cancellation on in-process restart (SIGUSR1). The fix adds: (1) post-sleep abort check (re-check `pendingChannelReloadAborted` after poll sleep, before `totalActive` check), (2) pre-start abort check (check `pendingChannelReloadAborted` between `stopChannel` and `startChannel`), (3) authorized-only abort (only call `abortPendingChannelReloads` after SIGUSR1 authorization passes).

**Real environment tested**: Linux / OpenClaw Gateway with OPENCLAW_NO_RESPAWN=1 (in-process restart mode) / SIGUSR1 signal

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
```bash
kill -SIGUSR1 $(cat /tmp/gateway.pid)
sleep 30
cat /tmp/gateway-94964.ip.log
```

**After-fix evidence**:
```
2026-06-24T17:51:46.890+08:00 [gateway] loading configuration…
2026-06-24T17:51:53.922+08:00 [gateway] agent model: stepfun-plan/step-3.5-flash
2026-06-24T17:51:53.924+08:00 [gateway] http server listening (13 plugins; 6.9s)
2026-06-24T17:51:53.929+08:00 [gateway] starting channels and sidecars...
2026-06-24T17:51:57.835+08:00 [gateway] ready
2026-06-24T17:52:36.808+08:00 [gateway] signal SIGUSR1 received
2026-06-24T17:52:36.813+08:00 [gateway] received SIGUSR1; restarting
2026-06-24T17:52:37.401+08:00 [feishu] feishu[default]: abort signal received, stopping
2026-06-24T17:52:37.446+08:00 [gateway] restart mode: in-process restart (OPENCLAW_NO_RESPAWN)
2026-06-24T17:52:37.451+08:00 [gateway] waiting for active work to drain before channel reload...
2026-06-24T17:52:37.451+08:00 [feishu] feishu[default]: received abort, cancelling deferred channel reload
2026-06-24T17:52:37.451+08:00 [gateway] deferred channel reload aborted; in-process restart in progress
2026-06-24T17:52:37.695+08:00 [gateway] abortPendingChannelReloads cleared all pending reloads
```

**Observed result after the fix**: SIGUSR1 triggers in-process restart; deferred channel reload is correctly cancelled ("deferred channel reload aborted") instead of racing to start channels. No EADDRINUSE or crash loop.

**What was not tested**: Fresh-start (non-reload) gateway initialization scenarios; concurrent SIGUSR1 signals arriving faster than 500ms poll interval.
## Tests and validation

### Unit tests

```
$ pnpm vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/backend-config.test.ts
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  28 passed (28)
```

### Integration / E2E

The lazy import in `restart.ts` gracefully handles cases where the reload handler module is not loaded (minimal/test gateways). The abort flag is a simple boolean — no new dependencies, no config changes, no API changes.

## Risk checklist

- [x] This change is backwards compatible — the abort flag defaults to false; reload behavior is unchanged unless an in-process restart fires
- [x] This change has been tested with existing configurations — all 28 related tests pass
- [x] I have updated relevant documentation — no API/config changes to document
- [x] Breaking changes (if any) are documented in Summary — N/A, no breaking changes

## Current review state

- [x] Self-review completed
- [ ] At least one maintainer review
- [ ] All CI checks passed
target.author: lzyyzznl

commands.review_summary:
The branch replaces the document-extract PDF image fallback’s single multi-page `clawpdf` call with one image extraction call per selected page and updates extractor tests for that call shape.

PR surface: Source +13, Tests +26. Total +39 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main batches fallback image extraction, and published `clawpdf@0.3.0` shares one `remainingPixels` budget across pages in that call. The PR body also includes before/after terminal-style output from a real 3-page render.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining action is maintainer acceptance or documentation of the changed `maxPixels` semantics, not a mechanical code repair.

target.title: fix(document-extract): render PDF image fallback per page so multi-page scans don't starve later pages
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Closes #96389.

When a non-native model processes a scanned/image-based PDF, `document-extract`
extracts no text and falls back to rendering each page to a PNG for vision OCR.
That fallback issued a single `clawpdf` call:

```js
pdf.extract({ mode: "images", maxPages, image: { maxPixels, ... } })
```

`clawpdf`'s image renderer applies `maxPixels` as **one budget shared across the
whole call** (`remainingPixels` is decremented after every page). On a multi-page
scan the first page consumes the budget and later pages are rendered with a
near-zero remaining budget — collapsing them to ~1×1 PNGs. Downstream vision
models then reject them, e.g.:

```
InternalError.Algo.InvalidParameter: The image length and width do not meet the model restrictions. [height:1...]
```

## Why This Change Was Made

The shared-budget behavior lives in the `clawpdf` dependency
(`node_modules/clawpdf/dist/extract.js` → `renderImages`), but the right owner
boundary for the fix is how this plugin *calls* clawpdf: each page in a fallback
render must be a usable image, so the pixel budget is meant to bound per-page
render quality, not be split across pages. Rendering each selected page in its
own `extract()` call resets `remainingPixels` per page while keeping clawpdf's
`maxPixels`/`maxDimension` capping and `forms` handling intact (no
reimplementation of the render budget in the plugin).

## User Impact

Multi-page scanned/image PDFs now render every selected page at the full
per-page pixel budget instead of starving later pages to degenerate images, so
vision OCR succeeds on the whole document. No config or API surface changes;
single-page behavior is unchanged.

## Evidence

Unit tests (`extensions/document-extract/document-extractor.test.ts`, 7 passing)
updated to assert each fallback page renders in its own `extract({ mode: "images",
pages: [n], ... })` call with the full per-page budget.

Real-behavior proof against the actual `clawpdf` 0.3.0 dependency — a 3-page
US-Letter PDF rendered at dpi=200 with `maxPixels=1,000,000`:

```
OLD (shared budget, one extract call):
  page 1: 879x1137  (999423 px)
  page 2: 21x27     (567 px)      <- starved
  page 3: 3x3       (9 px)        <- collapsed, rejected by vision models

NEW (per-page extract call):
  page 1: 879x1137  (999423 px)
  page 2: 879x1137  (999423 px)
  page 3: 879x1137  (999423 px)
```

Test run (worktree-safe runner):

```
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/document-extract/document-extractor.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  7 passed (7)
```

AI-assisted.
